overview: Secret Hitler is a dramatic game of political intrigue and betrayal. Players are secretly divided into Liberals and Fascists. The Fascists know each other, but the Liberals do not know anyone's role. Liberals must enact liberal policies or find and assassinate Hitler. Fascists try to enact fascist policies or get Hitler elected Chancellor.

roles.name: Liberal
roles.desc: You are on the Liberal team. Work together to enact 5 Liberal policies or identify and assassinate Hitler. You don't know anyone's role.

roles.name: Fascist
roles.desc: You know who Hitler is and who the other Fascists are. Sabotage the Liberals by getting fascist policies enacted and protecting Hitler.

roles.name: Hitler
roles.desc: You are the secret leader of the Fascists. In games with 5-6 players, you know who the Fascists are. In larger games, you don't. Stay hidden and try to get elected Chancellor.

phases.name: Nomination
phases.desc: The current President nominates a Chancellor candidate. They cannot nominate the previous President or Chancellor (term limits).

phases.name: Election
phases.desc: All living players vote Ja (yes) or Nein (no) on the proposed government. A majority is needed to pass.

phases.name: Legislative Session
phases.desc: If elected, the President draws 3 policy cards, discards one secretly, and passes 2 to the Chancellor. The Chancellor then enacts one of the two. No communication about the cards is allowed.

phases.name: Executive Action
phases.desc: Some fascist policy slots grant the President a special power: Investigate Loyalty, Call Special Election, Policy Peek, or Execution.

phases.name: Chaos
phases.desc: If 3 elections fail in a row, the top policy card is automatically enacted. Term limits are reset.

winConditions: Liberals win by enacting 5 Liberal policies.
Liberals win by assassinating Hitler (via Executive Action).
Fascists win by enacting 6 Fascist policies.
Fascists win if Hitler is elected Chancellor after 3+ Fascist policies are enacted.
tips: As a Liberal, pay attention to who conflicts with whom during legislative sessions — if the President says they passed two Liberal cards but a Fascist was enacted, someone is lying.
As a Fascist, create confusion. Blame Liberals for fascist policies.
As Hitler, play it safe early. Act like a Liberal to gain trust.
Track voting patterns — consistent Nein voters may be trying to block good governments.
The veto power becomes available after 5 Fascist policies are enacted. Both President and Chancellor must agree to veto.
Requires 5-8 players in this simplified version.
